title: ExportDialog UX: focus trap, Escape, ARIA, overlay click, file picker, 3MF removal
analysis:
This task addresses 9 UX issues in the ExportDialog modal. The current ExportDialog.tsx is a simple SolidJS component using <Show when={props.open}> for visibility. It renders an overlay div with an inner dialog div containing a format selector (STEP/STL/3MF), Cancel/Export buttons, and a text-only progress indicator.

Key findings from code analysis:
1. **No ARIA attributes**: The dialog div lacks role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby. The label has no htmlFor, the select has no id.
2. **No keyboard handling**: No Escape key handler, no focus trap. Tab freely escapes the modal.
3. **No overlay dismiss**: Clicking the overlay background doesn't close the dialog.
4. **3MF unsupported**: The backend ExportFormat enum only has Step/Stl/Obj (no 3MF). The Tauri command handler (commands.rs:54-57) explicitly rejects unknown formats. The frontend ExportFormat type includes '3mf' but it will always fail.
5. **Hardcoded path**: App.tsx handleDoExport (line 182) uses `export.${format}` as a hardcoded path. No native file picker.
6. **No spinner**: Progress indicator (line 22-25) is text-only 'Exporting...' with no visual spinner.
7. **Format persistence**: The createSignal<ExportFormat>('step') is at component level, so format SHOULD persist across open/close since the component is always mounted in App.tsx. But the task reports it resets — this may be a SolidJS <Show> subtlety or a perceived issue. Will add regression test.

Testing stack: vitest + @solidjs/testing-library + jsdom. All Tauri APIs are mocked via vi.mock. Existing ExportDialog tests cover basic rendering, format selection, button clicks, and disabled states.

For the file picker (E-6), I'll add a `pickSavePath` function to bridge.ts that wraps `@tauri-apps/plugin-dialog`'s save() function. In tests, this is mocked. App.tsx handleDoExport will call pickSavePath first, and if the user cancels (returns null), skip the export.

prerequisites.description: Add @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog to gui/package.json dependencies for native save file picker. Run npm/pnpm install to ensure it's available for import in bridge.ts.

steps.description: Write failing tests for ARIA attributes (E-4): (1) inner dialog div has role='dialog', (2) inner dialog div has aria-modal='true', (3) inner dialog div has aria-labelledby matching the title element's id. These test that the dialog element has proper accessibility semantics.
Add ARIA attributes to ExportDialog.tsx: add id='export-dialog-title' to the h2 title, add role='dialog', aria-modal='true', aria-labelledby='export-dialog-title' to the inner .dialog div.
Write failing test for label-select association (E-5): the <label> element has a for/htmlFor attribute whose value matches the <select> element's id attribute.
Add id='export-format-select' to the <select> element and for='export-format-select' (htmlFor in JSX) to the <label> element in ExportDialog.tsx.
Write failing test for Escape key closes dialog (E-2): render open dialog, fire keyDown event with key='Escape' on the dialog, assert onClose was called once.

Add onKeyDown handler to the overlay div in ExportDialog.tsx that checks for e.key === 'Escape' and calls props.onClose() when not exporting. Ensure the overlay is focusable or the handler is on document level.
Write failing tests for overlay click dismiss (E-3): (1) clicking the overlay background calls onClose, (2) clicking inside the inner dialog div does NOT call onClose (event propagation is stopped).

Add onClick handler to the overlay div that calls props.onClose() when not exporting. Add onClick={e => e.stopPropagation()} to the inner .dialog div to prevent clicks inside from bubbling to the overlay.

Write failing tests for focus trap (E-1): (1) when dialog opens, the first focusable element (Cancel button or select) receives focus, (2) pressing Tab on the last focusable element cycles focus back to the first focusable element, (3) pressing Shift+Tab on the first focusable element cycles to the last.

Implement focus trap in ExportDialog.tsx: use a ref on the inner dialog div. On mount (inside a createEffect triggered by props.open becoming true), query all focusable elements and focus the first one. Add a keydown handler that intercepts Tab/Shift+Tab and wraps focus within the dialog's focusable elements.

Write failing test for 3MF removal (I7): (1) format selector does NOT contain a '3mf' option, (2) format selector contains only 'step' and 'stl' options. Update the existing test 'format selector has STEP/STL/3MF options' to expect only STEP/STL.

Remove the <option value='3mf'>3MF</option> from ExportDialog.tsx. Update ExportFormat type in gui/src/types.ts from 'step' | 'stl' | '3mf' to 'step' | 'stl'. Verify no other frontend code references '3mf'.

Write failing test for CSS spinner during export (E-8): when exporting=true, the progress indicator contains an element with a spinner class (data-testid='export-spinner' or class check). The spinner element should be present only during export.

Add a spinner element inside the progress div in ExportDialog.tsx — a <span> with class styles.spinner. Add CSS @keyframes spin animation in ExportDialog.module.css: .spinner { width: 16px; height: 16px; border: 2px solid; border-top-color: transparent; border-radius: 50%; animation: spin 0.8s linear infinite; }

Write test for format persistence across open/close (E-7): render dialog, change format to 'stl', close dialog (set open=false), reopen (set open=true), assert select value is still 'stl'. This verifies the signal persists since the component stays mounted.

If step-15 test fails: lift the format signal to be preserved across Show renders, or restructure so the signal outlives the Show block. If test passes, this step is a no-op (format already persists due to component-level signal). Mark done either way.

Write failing tests in App.test.tsx for file picker integration (E-6): (1) mock bridge.pickSavePath to return a path string, click Export in dialog, assert pickSavePath was called with expected default name and format filters, then assert bridgeExportGeometry was called with the returned path. (2) mock pickSavePath to return null (user cancelled), assert bridgeExportGeometry was NOT called and dialog stays open.

Add pickSavePath(defaultName: string, formatExtension: string) function to bridge.ts that imports save() from @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog. Returns Promise<string | null>. Modify handleDoExport in App.tsx: before calling bridgeExportGeometry, call pickSavePath to get the user-chosen path. If null (cancelled), return early without exporting. Pass the chosen path to bridgeExportGeometry instead of the hardcoded default.

design_decisions.decision: Focus trap implemented manually rather than using a third-party library
design_decisions.rationale: The project has no focus-trap library (e.g., focus-trap-react). The ExportDialog has only 3 focusable elements (select, Cancel, Export), making a manual implementation trivial and avoiding a new dependency. Query focusable elements via dialog.querySelectorAll('button, select, input, [tabindex]').

design_decisions.decision: Escape key handler placed on the overlay div via onKeyDown rather than a document-level listener
design_decisions.rationale: A component-level handler is cleaner and automatically scoped to the dialog's lifecycle. The overlay receives focus management from the focus trap, so keyDown events will bubble up to it. This avoids global listener registration/cleanup complexity.

design_decisions.decision: pickSavePath abstracted in bridge.ts rather than importing @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog directly in App.tsx
design_decisions.rationale: All Tauri IPC interactions go through bridge.ts (existing pattern). This keeps App.tsx decoupled from Tauri specifics and makes testing straightforward — tests mock bridge.pickSavePath rather than the plugin directly.

design_decisions.decision: Remove 3MF from frontend ExportFormat type entirely rather than disabling the option
design_decisions.rationale: The backend ExportFormat enum has Step/Stl/Obj but NO 3MF variant. The Tauri command handler rejects unknown format strings. Showing a disabled 3MF option would confuse users. Clean removal is better — it can be added back when backend support exists.

design_decisions.decision: Overlay click dismiss checks props.exporting before closing
design_decisions.rationale: Consistent with Cancel button behavior — clicking overlay while export is in progress should not close the dialog and potentially leave the export in an ambiguous state.

design_decisions.decision: CSS-only spinner animation rather than SVG or animated icon library
design_decisions.rationale: No icon library is in the project dependencies. A CSS border-animation spinner is lightweight, theme-compatible (uses CSS variables), and sufficient for the simple 'Exporting...' state indicator.

reuse.what: Existing ExportDialog test patterns (render with props, fireEvent.click, screen.getByTestId)
reuse.how: Follow the exact same render pattern: render(() => <ExportDialog open={true} exporting={false} onExport={vi.fn()} onClose={vi.fn()} />). Use fireEvent for keyboard and click events. Use screen queries for assertions.

reuse.what: App.test.tsx mock patterns for Tauri APIs and bridge module
reuse.how: Add pickSavePath to the existing bridge mock block (vi.mock('../bridge')). Follow the same mockResolvedValue pattern. Add @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog mock alongside existing @tauri-apps/api mocks if needed.
Add pickSavePath following the same async function pattern with JSDoc comment. Import from @tauri-apps/plugin-dialog instead of @tauri-apps/api/core since it's a separate plugin.
Use existing CSS variable names (--reify-accent, --reify-subtext, etc.) for spinner colors. Follow the existing class naming pattern (.spinner alongside .progress, .button, etc.).

reuse.what: Existing SolidJS createEffect/onMount patterns for lifecycle hooks
reuse.how: Use createEffect to react to props.open changes for focus management. Use onCleanup for any event listener cleanup. Follow the same import pattern from 'solid-js'.
